key_insight: MobileNetV3 can accurately classify pipeline weld defects from noisy RGB images while remaining lightweight enough for on-device inspection.
engineering_relevance: The method supports faster, lower-power, and more portable weld inspection workflows, making automated quality control more practical in field conditions.
method: Preprocess and augment weld images to improve robustness to noisy acquisition conditions.
Train and compare MobileNetV3-Small and MobileNetV3-Large for multi-class defect classification.
Target deployment on mobile/handheld platforms via TensorFlow Lite, ONNX, and CoreML.
evidence_signal: The study was evaluated on two practical datasets: AI5083 with 4,927 HDR-captured images and PWDID with 78 mobile-phone images, achieving 99.99% accuracy on AI5083 and about a 3% improvement over prior methods.
watch_item: Performance on the smaller, uncontrolled PWDID dataset may be less stable, so broader validation across more real-world inspection conditions is still needed.
paper_title: MobileNetV3 for Pipeline Welding Defect Detection: A Computer Vision Approach on Noisy RGB Images
